title: coreopsis guanajuatensis
intro: Coreopsis guanajuatensis, also known as Mexican tickseed, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Coreopsis guanajuatensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Coreopsis guanajuatensis is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cases, it may be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
